title: Ctenitis biformis
intro: Ctenitis biformis is a species of fern native to Asia, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Research has shown that Ctenitis biformis possesses several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis biformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ctenitis biformis is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Ctenitis biformis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is part of the genus Ctenitis which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Ctenitis biformis reflects its evolutionary relationships to other ferns in the family Dryopteridaceae. The scientific classification of Ctenitis biformis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Ctenitis, and species Ctenitis biformis. The classification of Ctenitis biformis helps scientists to understand its characteristics and evolutionary history.
